Dean Graziosi: Now, we have examples for you, but this could be up here, the first couple ask
any information you want about them, right. Where they live, what their
business is, how much money they do in their business. You could go as little or
as deep as you want.
Dean Graziosi: I love getting their name, their phone number, their email, the name of their
business. It depends on what your outcome is, right, if they're in business, you
might wanna know how much revenue they currently doing, what's the name of
their company, what kind of business are they in? You could just ask a couple of
those quick questions, but me, I like more of the qualifying questions to see if
they're a fit.
Dean Graziosi: So, let me give you some examples of questions I've used through the years on
these exact pages. This isn't something that's hearsay, it's not something that
sounds like a good idea. These are exact questions, I had my assistant print off
past pages that we've used. So I've asked questions such as, "Are you a driven
person who will see challenges through to the end and not easily give up?"
Dean Graziosi: I don't want people coming to think they're gonna cut a check and be rich
overnight for doing nothing. I want people that follow through, that take action.
I don't want the room in myself to share and then they go home and do nothing
and go, "That mastermind didn't work." I want that kind of person, so I ask that.
Dean Graziosi: Here's another question, and that would go in here, "Are you willing to commit
an appropriate amount of time to learning and growing your business, to fixing
the relationship, to losing the weight, to expanding your company, to scale, to
learn to sell," whatever it is that you do, "To date better, to be a better parent,"
whatever it is that you do. I like asking, "Are you willing to commit?" I don't
want dabblers, I want people who are ready.
Dean Graziosi: Another question I've asked is, because I do all my masterminds right here in my
office, I always say, "Would you be willing to jump on a plane and fly down to
my personal office for one or two full days and commit and be totally dedicated
to your next level?"
Dean Graziosi: Some people might say, "No," but maybe you're doing a virtual one so you don't
need that, so you might say, "Would you be willing to commit a half a day
virtually online without answering your phone, without being distracted, like
hidden some place so you can actually absorb the knowledge I'm sharing?" I like
people who are ready to go.
Dean Graziosi: Another one, you might ask, "What's your number one goal?" Or, "What would
be the ideal outcome you'd have for attending an event like this?" Or, "What is
the number one challenge you're facing right now in your business, your life, in
your weight, in your relationship?" Get that information, get thinking through
that. And I've asked other questions like, "What's your main motivation for
wanting to join this mastermind?"

Dean Graziosi: And then what I did that I never used to do, in here, I left a voice memo. Not a

that. This is how. I see your biggest opportunity is X," and I would just lean into
one thing, and I would ... because I know they wrote down every ... what their
fears are, what their goals are, what's been holding them back.
Dean Graziosi: And they're qualified, so I would build rapport through a two to three minute

"There's 50 spots," and be honest, "Half of 'em are full. 20% of 'em are full. So

and I have a couple more for you to see if you're absolutely qualified."
Dean Graziosi: Did you hear that? You're not saying, "Hey, I'm gonna call you to try to sell you. I
have a couple more questions to see if you're qualified and be a perfect fit for
the group. Just text me back and let me know a time." Immediately, every one I
did, in the hundred thousand dollar group, I did all these in a week. I'd get a text

can fit me in. I hope I'm the perfect fit. I hope that there's a seat for me."
Literally almost all of them said that.

Dean Graziosi: So I'm gonna tell you what I do. So I get on the phone and say, "Hey, Diane. How

questions on your side." And so many times like, "I just hope I can get in," or "I

Dean Graziosi: And sometimes they say, "Are you gonna teach marketing?" "Oh, that's my main
focus." "Oh, so good. Are you gonna teach standing operating procedures?"
"Nah, that's not my thing. I scale through marketing, kinda the art side, the
science side, that's not where we're gonna go deep on that. I'll bring in some
experts, but that's not me. Like this is the part," and then I'll say, "Okay, glad I
got those questions out of the way." So, tell 'em you're gonna say something,
say, "Oh, before that, let me ask you if you have any questions." Answer those
questions, and then go to this.
Dean Graziosi: You ready? "Listen, I'd love to have you in a group, but I wanna make sure it's a
fit for both of us. I wanna make sure you get so much value that you can't stop
bragging about this for the next 10 years. So let me ask you, if it was a year from
today and we were together, we were back on the phone. You joined, you got
one of those rare seats in the mastermind, and it was a year from now, and
we're on the phone talking about your year, and it was the best year of your life.
You were making the money, you lost the weight, you fixed the relationship,
your practice doubled, you got the guy, you got the girl," whatever it is.
Dean Graziosi: If that was the ... "What would be the ideal outcome one year from today, if it
was the best year of your life?" And listen. And they'll tell ya, "Oh, my sales, my
marketing, hiring," they will tell you what they want. And in your head, you can
decide can you help 'em achieve it? If you can't, I'm telling you be ethical and
push them away. If they say, "I need this, I need someone to come in with a
million bucks and a franchise," and you know nothing about raising money in a
franchise, push them away.
Dean Graziosi: But as they tell you, they'll start to dream. Let them dream. If you can help 'em,
the next question will anchor it. "All right, Diane, that was amazing. I really think
that this group and myself can help you achieve that goal and overcome the
obstacles along the way. But I got one last question for ya: why do you think you
should get one of these rare seats? Or why do you think you should be in this
